hawkish_phrases: some tightening of monetary policy would be required to achieve a sustainable return of inflation to the target
monetary policy could need to be tightened by a somewhat greater extent over the forecast period than the path implied by the yield curve
Two members considered it appropriate to increase Bank Rate by 25 basis points
inflation is projected to remain above the MPC's target throughout the forecast period
CPI inflation rose to 2.6% in June...expects inflation to rise further in coming months and to peak around 3% in October
dovish_phrases: GDP growth remains sluggish in the near term as the squeeze on households' real incomes continues to weigh on consumption
Attempting to offset fully the effect of weaker sterling on inflation would be achievable only at the cost of higher unemployment
any increases in Bank Rate would be expected to be at a gradual pace and to a limited extent
six members thought that the current policy stance remained appropriate
the economy operates with a small degree of spare capacity
summary: The MPC voted 6-2 to hold Bank Rate at 0.25%, with two members dissenting in favor of a 25bp hike, while the Committee signaled that some tightening would be required and monetary policy could need to be tightened by a somewhat greater extent than market pricing.
